text: um imagine just for a moment that you have reached the absolute peak of your success right
the very top yeah really think about it everything you have ever worked for uh every long night
every single struggle or obstacle you had to face it is all behind you now everything is finally
yours you're perfectly comfortable exactly your enemies are defeated you are powerful and your
life is completely secure it sounds like the perfect dream oh absolutely it's what everybody
wants but what if i told you that this exact moment of triumph is actually the moment you
were in the greatest spiritual and moral danger what if the time when you feel you know the most
secure is exactly when your character is the most vulnerable to just completely collapsing that is
um that is such a fascinating thought and it really goes against almost everything we are
taught today it really does we usually think of danger as something that happens when we are weak
or when we are losing or you know when we are at
the bottom of the mountain we rarely think of success itself as a dangerous thing right
success is supposed to be the safe zone exactly but history and especially ancient history shows
us over and over again that extreme success can actually blind us it can make us forget who we
are it can make us forget where we came from yeah and it can make us trust in our own power instead
of the things that actually matter and that beautiful kind of terrifying tension brings
us right to our mission for today welcome to the deep
dive today we are doing something a little bit different and i am uh i'm just so excited about
it me too this is a great topic we are going completely microscopic today we are going to
put a massive magnifying glass over one single fascinating chapter of ancient history we are
looking exclusively at chapter 24 of the book of 2 samuel just that one chapter just that one we
